design_decisions.d0r_pending_approval: D0.R made 0 assumption + 1 definition change(s) PROVISIONALLY (pending approval, direction-of-truth align/correct/discharge): def:branch-map-contact-handle
design_decisions.d0_open_oeq_residuals: Open-ended question(s) left as the note's acknowledged open problem for D0.5 review: oeq:full-branch-optimizer-map

added_assumptions.label: ass:homogeneous-linear-share-working-model
added_assumptions.statement: The specialized linear-interference working model generating the leading-variance expansion for hat_tau_DM is homogeneous across clusters and affine in the centered share d = u - pbar, with cluster-invariant working-model coefficients in the block-fixed isolated-cluster specialization.

proposed_from.topic: Study optimal saturation design for two-stage randomized-saturation experiments under isolated partial interference by making the full bounded-support moment program the theorem object: under a specialized within-cluster homogeneous linear-in-share working model (a specialization of Cai, Pouget-Abadie and Airoldi 2022, not the full parent model), minimize V(nu)=V0+V1*m2+V3*m3+V4*(m4-m2^2) over laws nu on [0,1] with mean pbar. Prove the main result as a necessary-and-sufficient quartic dual/KKT global-optimality certificate for any candidate law, with a support-at-most-3 optimizer reduction. Only as a scoped corollary, in the positive-penalty symmetric-candidate regime V1>0,V4>0, characterize when the stratified symmetric law delta_pbar is globally optimal by the closed threshold min_{d in [-pbar,1-pbar]}(V1+V3*d+V4*d^2)>=0; when this fails construct the skewed two/three-point optimizer. Include the witness pbar=1/3,V1=1,V4=1,V3=-10: (2/3)delta_0+(1/3)delta_1 has m2=2/9,m3=2/27,m4-m2^2=2/81 and improves by -40/81 over delta_pbar. Recover the SUTVA corner V3=V4=0: V is linear in m2, so V1>0 gives within-cluster stratified delta_pbar, V1<0 gives cluster Bernoulli (1-pbar)delta_0+pbar delta_1, and V1=0 ties. Add finite-M attainability for denominator-compatible support masses/saturations and an O(1/M+1/m) rounding-loss clause, distinguishing M*pbar integrality for cluster Bernoulli vertices from m*pbar integrality for within-cluster stratified saturation.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"chosen_seed":"profiled_quartic_global_certificate","closest_published_results":[{"bibkey":"CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022","published_result":"Closed-form bias and variance under linear interference with optimization over symmetric randomized and deterministic saturation families.","precise_gap":"Does not deliver a necessary-and-sufficient global optimality certificate over all bounded-support laws with fixed mean, does not isolate the support-at-most-three optimizer reduction for the actual nonconvex objective, and does not state the branch-correct iff threshold in the positive-penalty regime."},{"bibkey":"BairdBohrenMcIntoshOzler2017","published_result":"Finite-menu randomized-saturation design and power tradeoffs under partial interference.","precise_gap":"Works over prespecified saturation menus rather than arbitrary laws on [0,1], so it does not solve the unrestricted moment program or its exact finite implementation problem."},{"bibkey":"Winkler1988","published_result":"Extreme-point geometry for linear moment problems.","precise_gap":"Handles the inner linear slice after conditioning on m2 but does not absorb the outer -V4 m2(nu)^2 term into a global saturation-design certificate."}],"why_not_subsumed":"The Step 1 scan across the anchor, its adjacent randomized-saturation papers, and the archive found no closer result with a quartic iff certificate, no published branch-correct symmetry threshold, and no exact finite-M attainability plus O(1/M+1/m) rounding guarantee for this objective. The archived failures also show that dropping the nonlinear residual or using a universal skew threshold is not a minor presentation issue but the core unsolved obstruction."}

seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Give a necessary-and-sufficient global optimality certificate for the full bounded-support randomized-saturation moment program with objective V0+V1*m2+V3*m3+V4*(m4-m2^2).
seed_details.seed: Profile the nonlinear m2 term, prove a quartic dual/KKT iff certificate for global optimality over all laws nu on [0,1] with mean pbar, and deduce existence of a support-at-most-three optimizer.
seed_details.red_team: Immediate from Winkler after conditioning on m2.
seed_details.rebuttal: Winkler handles the inner linear slice only; the unresolved content is the outer nonconvex profiling that keeps the -V4 m2(nu)^2 residual in the global certificate.

seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Characterize exactly when the within-cluster stratified law delta_pbar is globally optimal in the positive-penalty symmetric-candidate regime, and construct the skewed optimizer when it is not.
seed_details.seed: On the branch V1>0, V4>0, prove that delta_pbar is globally optimal iff min_{d in [-pbar,1-pbar]}(V1+V3*d+V4*d^2) >= 0, and otherwise construct the improving skewed two-point or three-point law.
seed_details.red_team: Only a local perturbation inequality around delta_pbar.
seed_details.rebuttal: The point is to derive the inequality from the global quartic certificate, not from a local directional derivative, and then pair it with a constructive non-symmetric optimizer when the branch fails.

seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Convert an optimal continuous saturation law into an exact or rounded finite two-stage randomized-saturation design while separating cluster-count and within-cluster denominator constraints.
seed_details.seed: For support-at-most-three optimizers, prove exact denominator-compatible finite-M implementation and an O(1/M+1/m) loss bound for grid-split rounding, with distinct integrality conditions for Bernoulli vertices and stratified saturation.
seed_details.red_team: This is only a rounding lemma once the optimizer is known.
seed_details.rebuttal: The literature and archive both miss the split between cluster-mass and within-cluster denominator compatibility; the theorem certifies optimizer preservation rather than ad hoc approximation.

seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Show that the skew-aware saturation moment program recovers the no-interference SUTVA simplex-vertex rule as an exact degenerate corner, including the correct finite integrality clauses.
seed_details.seed: Use the same profiled certificate to recover the V3=V4=0 corner and the exact finite integrality distinction between the Bernoulli vertex and delta_pbar.
seed_details.red_team: Already known from Cai under SUTVA.
seed_details.rebuttal: As a standalone kernel it is too incremental; it survives only as a sanity corollary inside the stronger interference theorem.

seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Give a necessary-and-sufficient global optimality certificate for the full bounded-support randomized-saturation moment program with objective V0+V1*m2+V3*m3+V4*(m4-m2^2).
seed_details.seed: Derive a coefficient frontier separating parameter regions where every optimizer can be chosen two-point from regions where three-point contact is unavoidable.
seed_details.red_team: Routine case-splitting after the support-at-most-three reduction.
seed_details.rebuttal: It is not published, but it looks secondary relative to the global certificate that would generate it.

seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Give a necessary-and-sufficient global optimality certificate for the full bounded-support randomized-saturation moment program with objective V0+V1*m2+V3*m3+V4*(m4-m2^2).
seed_details.seed: Characterize when the global randomized optimizer is already deterministic and when genuine randomization strictly lowers the variance objective.
seed_details.red_team: Cai already compares randomized and deterministic saturation designs.
seed_details.rebuttal: The equality frontier is not explicit there, but the seed is still downstream of the stronger unrestricted law theorem.

seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Characterize exactly when the within-cluster stratified law delta_pbar is globally optimal in the positive-penalty symmetric-candidate regime, and construct the skewed optimizer when it is not.
seed_details.seed: Classify the V3=0 benchmark for all sign patterns of (V1,V4) so that every later skew threshold is benchmarked against the correct zero-skew optimizer.
seed_details.rebuttal: Necessary for soundness, but not strong enough to carry the angle alone.

seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Determine which departures from isolated homogeneous within-cluster linear-in-share interference preserve a finite-dimensional saturation-moment optimal-design certificate.
seed_details.seed: Classify the weakest departures from isolated homogeneous within-cluster interference that still reduce optimal design to finitely many saturation moments, and prove counterexamples when additional state variables are unavoidable.
seed_details.red_team: Too broad for one paper and likely underpowered without a tight negative theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: Potentially interesting, but it is a pivot seed rather than the best cold-start angle for this qid.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:anonymous-share-specialization / ass:homogeneous-linear-share-working-model
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The Cai-style homogeneous linear-in-share variance representation is treated as conditional setup rather than as the novelty contribution.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The global certificate, threshold, witness, and finite implementation claims are field-tier claims conditional on V(nu)=V_0+V_1 m_2+V_3 m_3+V_4(m_4-m_2^2).

banked_reason: Sound abstract quartic-program optimization theory (general V), but the saturation-skew DESIGN claim is degenerate under the run's homogeneous working model: it forces V3=V4=0, so the design variance has no skew dependence (paper-confirmed vs Cai arXiv:2203.09682; Lean-confirmed). Formalization halted at F2/F3 when stopped. Re-raise via heterogeneous (unit-level gamma_i) model + L7/L8 finite-population permutation-moment substrate.
